name: Formation
description_short: Formation Federal Credit Union is a Baton Rouge-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with member-focused service and federal deposit insurance.
description_long:
Formation Federal Credit Union has roots dating back to the late 1930s when employees of Ethyl Corporation established the Ethyl Corporation Credit Union. The institution expanded membership beyond its original employee base in 1981 and has grown to include over 350 Select Employee Groups (SEGs) by 1996. It rebranded as Louisiana USA Federal Credit Union to reflect its diversity, and most recently changed its name to Formation Federal Credit Union in 2025. After 80 years of operation, the credit union maintains financial soundness and strong capitalization.

Formation offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including dividends-bearing checking accounts, savings accounts, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and credit cards with multiple design options. Members can access online account login, make payments, apply for loans and mortgages, and manage their finances through digital channels. The credit union emphasizes its people-first philosophy, stating "we're for people instead of profit," which reflects the cooperative structure typical of credit unions.

The institution distinguishes itself through 75+ years of service experience, a professional staff with decades of combined expertise, and nationwide access via Shared Branching and CO-OP ATM networks allowing members to complete transactions across the United States. Member deposits are federally insured up to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund, with account structures allowing up to $1,250,000 in aggregate insured deposits per member. The credit union positions itself as a personal, consistent, and dedicated alternative to traditional banks.

Formation is a legitimate financial institution regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration). However, as a regional credit union primarily serving the Baton Rouge area, membership may be restricted to specific employee groups or geographic communities. The website lacks transparency regarding membership eligibility requirements and detailed fee schedules are referenced but not fully displayed. Interest rates and loan terms are not prominently featured on the main pages reviewed.
services: Shared branching access across the United States
ATM locator and nationwide CO-OP ATM access
pros: Federal deposit insurance through NCUA up to $250,000 per member, with account structuring allowing $1,250,000 aggregate coverage
80+ years of operational history and financial stability with strong capitalization
Nationwide access to branches and ATMs through Shared Branching and CO-OP networks
Dividends-bearing checking accounts that allow members to earn interest on deposits
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, HELOCs, and credit cards
Member-focused cooperative structure emphasizing service over profit
Multiple credit card designs with rewards programs and flexible benefits
cons: Membership eligibility unclear from website; likely restricted to specific employee groups and SEG affiliations
Limited transparency on interest rates, APRs, and detailed fee schedules on main pages
Regional institution primarily serving Baton Rouge area, may have limited physical locations
No information provided about online-only membership or digital banking limitations
Loan application processes and approval timelines not clearly documented
best_for: Employees of member companies and Select Employee Groups (SEGs) in the Baton Rouge region
Individuals seeking member-owned financial institutions with dividend-bearing accounts
Borrowers needing auto loans, mortgages, or personal loans from a cooperative lender
Members wanting nationwide ATM and branch access through shared networks
diagnosis: Formation Federal Credit Union is best for employees of member companies and Select Employee Groups seeking a member-owned financial institution with comprehensive banking services, federal deposit insurance, and a cooperative structure. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to specific organizations and employee groups, making it inaccessible to the general public, and detailed fee and rate information is not prominently displayed on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Formation — Credit Union in Baton Rouge, LA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Formation — NCUA-insured credit union in Baton Rouge, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
